texto: An absolute money grab. Definitely not worth it though the beach at the end was nice. Attendants at the entrance are super rude
Love the hermit crabs!

Un lugar espectacular y, sin duda, una visita obligada para cualquiera que viaje a Manuel Antonio.

El parque está muy bien mantenido, los senderos principales son cómodos de recorrer y todo se encuentra en excelente estado. Nosotros realizamos únicamente el recorrido principal, ya que no tuvimos tiempo de explorar todos los senderos secundarios, pero la experiencia fue muy buena de principio a fin.

Las playas son, sencillamente, impresionantes. De hecho, creemos que una de ellas podría estar fácilmente entre las mejores playas de Costa Rica. El agua es tranquila, el entorno es hermoso y la combinación entre selva y playa crea un paisaje realmente único.

Nuestro principal consejo es llegar lo más temprano posible. Si lográs adelantarte a los grupos organizados, podés disfrutar varias horas con muy poca gente y vivir una experiencia mucho más tranquila. Nosotros estuvimos en temporada baja y durante la mañana la playa se disfrutaba muchísimo. Sin embargo, hacia el mediodía comenzaron a llegar más grupos y la cantidad de visitantes aumentó considerablemente. Imaginamos que durante la temporada alta esto debe ser aún más notorio.

Algo que sí consideramos importante mencionar ocurre antes de ingresar al parque. En los accesos hay varias personas que se acercan a los visitantes para ofrecer estacionamiento, guías o distintos servicios. Algunas incluso pueden dar la impresión de estar vinculadas al parque, lo que puede generar confusión para quienes visitan por primera vez.

Nuestra recomendación es informarse previamente sobre los servicios oficiales, no detenerse apresuradamente ante la primera persona que los llame y comparar opciones antes de decidir dónde estacionar o contratar una excursión. Nosotros tuvimos una experiencia incómoda intentando aclarar precios y condiciones, por lo que vale la pena tomarse unos minutos para verificar la información antes de aceptar cualquier servicio.

Más allá de eso, la experiencia dentro del parque fue excelente. Naturaleza espectacular, playas increíbles y senderos muy bien cuidados. Un lugar que definitivamente volveríamos a visitar.

El parque es espectacular, las 3 son porque esta tan mal el ingreso, donde gente se te rira arriba del vwhiculo para que estaciones. Lo note muy agresivo toda la entrada, como te quieren vender todo, pero de una manera bastante agresiva. Ojala el estado haga algo porque el parque no se merece ese ingreso.
Touristy and very overrated. Many other great parks ( not far) and places to see wildlife. Beaches great though.

Increíble!! Lleno de flora y fauna del país, con caminos fáciles de recorrer, distintas rutas y variedad de playas hermosas. Si estás de suerte podrás ver a los animales en su hábitat natural. Aparte del costo de la entrada al parque, si recomiendo contratar el paquete del guía ya que ellos conocen los nombres y muchas especies de animales y plantas lo cuál lo hace mucho más completo. Pura vida 💛 🤙
Amazing park. Hire a local guide of you can for an excellent experience

חוויה מזעזעת ומסוכנת. הגענו היום (3.6.2026) לפארק הלאומי מנואל אנטוניו, ונתקלנו ברשת של אנשים שלבשו ווסטים והתחזו לעובדי הפארק כדי להוציא כספים במרמה עבור חניה. כשהבנו שמדובר בנוכלות וסירבנו לשלם, המצב הפך לאלים: אחת הנשים שפועלת שם באזור חסמה את רכבנו בצורה אגרסיבית וניסתה לתקוף אותנו.
הגענו למקום אחרי נסיעה של 5 שעות ורכשנו כרטיסים מראש, אך נאלצנו לברוח מהאזור כי אין נוכחות משטרתית והרגשנו סכנה ממשית לביטחוננו.
אזהרה לכל המטיילים: אל תעצרו לאנשים שמכוונים אתכם לחניה מחוץ לחנייה הרשמית של הפארק ואל תפתחו חלונות. המקום הזה הפך למלכודת מסוכנת עבור תיירים, והרשויות בקוסטה ריקה חייבות לטפל בזה בדחיפות.
We had a fantastic time exploring the wildlife and enjoying the beautiful beach. Kenneth, our tour guide, made the experience even more enjoyable. 🦥🐒🦇🦀
Parque imperdível! O estacionamento fora do parque custa U$ 10

Fabuloso lugar para apreciar y tener un contacto con la naturaleza y fauna de Costa Rica, además la playa está increíble. 🥰💚
Es importante seguir las indicaciones de no ingresar comida, no tirar basura y  sobre todo No salirse de los senderos y no acercarse a ningún animal.

Take the tour once in ur life. Rest of urvlifeenjoy pay general admittance.or do what you want.
The playa/lagoon y other part with trails is wonderful place to get after informative tour. Take metal water container, not plastic container.  No food or gum allowed. Lockers you have to pay.
Mint nemzeti park nem nagy szám, eléggé lehangoló beton járdákkal. Inkább egy városi parkra hasonlít. Állatokat azért lehet látni, és az óceánparti strand miatt megéri kifizetni a belépőt.
Un guia muy amable, pero mucha gente visitando el parque

you can either take a tour guide or self guided. i did self guided, reservation made online, choose your entry time and thats it. go early like 7am, no goons asking for parking fees and you will have the park to yourself. we saw a troupe of capuchin monkeys walk just right next to us. Also has a nice beach where iguanas roost. the interior trail past the isthimus is where we saw the capuchin monkeys
Un lugar bonito para caminar y observar la naturaleza
Good park to see animals. But you need a guide to show you .

We were there end of May. In the offseason the park is nice and not so crowded. Embrace yourself however for a really terrible parking and getting there experience. The people (not official park staff) advice you where to park, before you can turn off your engine they rip the door open and really push you to pay for parking (10000 colonnes) and hiring them as a guide so they can show you around. And they really get aggressive and grumpy and dont take no for an answer. Very bad experience. Once you made it through them (and without them) to the park and you leave the main road in the park it is a beautiful experience with a lot to see and is worth the fight.

Nous avons réservé une visite avec guide privé et la prise en charge à l'hôtel directement via le Parc national Manuel Antonio.
La visite avec le guide et la découverte du parc se sont bien passées même si la visite se fait un peu au pas de course.
Je suis cependant vraiment déçue par le service. Après la visite nous avons repris le bus qui nous avait déposé au parc et lorsque nous sommes arrivés à l’hôtel, le chauffeur nous a réclamé 5 $ par personne, soit un total de 25 $ en nous disant que juste l'aller était inclus.
À aucun moment le prestataire ni le chauffeur ne nous ont dit que le trajet de retour serait facturé en supplément ni n’ont mentionné le prix.
Deux Uber nous auraient coûté deux fois moins cher.
Soyez prudents, c'est une vraie arnaque !
Good hike, but I recommend hiring a guide if you want to see a lot of birds. Went hoping to see the macaws, but didn't see any. You need to buy a ticket online and recommend you do it before arrival.

Be aware of scamers
Always buy the tickets before get to the park
There are a couple of people near the park asking if you have tickets
If don't have they will force you to  buy from them at the highest price,
I refuse to buy from them because they try to charge  3 times higher  then the price that was posted online,but before I make the u turn one the them scratch my car because  took a photo
Assolutamente merita una visita, anche due. Molto ben organizzato. Guide competenti.
Amazing place!  We had a wonderful guided tour and saw lots of animals, then spent the rest of the day at the beaches inside the park.  Absolutely wonderful!
פארק ענק עם המון חיות וחופים שקטים ויפים
Muy preciosas playas, muy cuidado y seguro

Warning for other tourists visiting Manuel Antonio National Park.

Before reaching the official entrance, we were stopped by unofficial “parking attendants” wearing fake official-looking gear and pressured into paying $20 cash for parking. They also aggressively tried to force us to hire a guide.

When I asked for a receipt, they could not provide one and just shrugged. The whole situation felt misleading and dishonest, especially because they acted as if they were part of the national park system.

Do not stop for random people on the road. Drive directly to the official entrance and be careful with anyone claiming guides or parking are mandatory.

Better to go to any other National Park!!!
Bring a swimsuit and towel so you can swim at the white sand beach!
Muy turístico, muchísimas gente, guías y ruido. No permite la observación tranquila de la naturaleza.  La playas hermosas.

Der Nationalpark Manuel Antonio in Costa Rica gehört zu den bekanntesten Nationalparks des Landes und ist vor allem dafür bekannt, dass er auf relativ kleiner Fläche sehr viel Abwechslung bietet. Besonders ist die Kombination aus dichtem Regenwald, tierreicher Natur und schönen Stränden, die direkt ineinander übergehen und sich gut an einem Tag erleben lassen.

Die Wege im Park sind gut ausgebaut und auch für normale Besucher ohne große Wandererfahrung gut machbar. Unterwegs kann man mit etwas Glück viele Tiere sehen, zum Beispiel Affen, Faultiere, Leguane und verschiedene Vogelarten. Genau diese Tierbeobachtungen machen den Park für viele so interessant.

Ein Highlight sind die Strände im Park. Sie sind sehr schön, mit hellem Sand und türkisfarbenem Wasser, und liegen direkt am Dschungel. Dadurch wirkt alles sehr ursprünglich und deutlich besonderer als viele klassische Badeorte.

Allerdings ist der Park auch außerhalb der Hauptsaison gut besucht. Selbst im Juni, also eigentlich in der Nebensaison, war es bei unserem Besuch schon ziemlich voll, sowohl auf den Wegen als auch an den Stränden. Das nimmt ein bisschen von der Ruhe, die man in einem Nationalpark eigentlich erwarten würde. Außerdem muss man Tickets meist im Voraus buchen, da der Eintritt begrenzt ist.

Insgesamt ist Manuel Antonio trotzdem ein sehr lohnenswertes Reiseziel, besonders wenn man zum ersten Mal in Costa Rica ist und Natur, Tiere und Strand in kurzer Zeit erleben möchte. Wer es allerdings ruhiger und weniger touristisch mag, sollte eher andere, weniger bekannte Parks in Betracht ziehen.
El mejor parque oídos senderos hermosa playa y mucha fauna y flora recomiendo no dejar de visitar
Súper encantada con este parque! Las playas son espectaculares y los animalitos que se ven son para volverse locooo!!!! Hermosa la experiencia

Must see for all animal lovers - early arrival and a guide is strongly recommended to take the most out of your experience. Inside the park a fantastic beach with a shower and changing space so plan accordingly

IMPORTANT INFO
There are two main things that you need to know if you are visiting this incredible park. First, pay the extra few dollars and hire a guide. It dramatically enhances the experience far more than you would ever imagine.
Secondly, request a guide named Beatriz. She is absolutely incredible in her job, and you will have a completely elevated experience as a result of her expertise. Without her, this park would have been a six. With her, it’s an easy 10! Without her, this experience would have been “kind of fun“, but with her, this tour became something that was truly unforgettable. The photos I’m attaching are actually from our tour with her. There’s no chance we would have been able to see things like this without her guidance.

This park was worth every bit of the effort made to get to it. Some things to add that may be helpful when planning your trip: Buy tickets in advance on the SINAC website and book for the earliest time possible (I did 7am). You cannot purchase at the location. There are bathrooms and showers throughout the park near the beaches. There’s a restaurant with a decent variety of foods, juices, ice cream, etc. which is wonderful since absolutely no food of any sort can be brought inside the park, not even chewing gum. Your bags will be searched upon entering. You don’t HAVE to have a guide to see wildlife, but if seeing sloths are the top of the list, you may consider using one. I just watched other groups with a guide and looked at where they were watching and was able to see one (usually up high in trees). Monkeys were in multiple areas, and I got to see them up close. If you do all the trails, it’s a little over 5 miles. I did them all in one day, and I’d recommend wearing good shoes and not just slide on shoes.

PARKING: People will be flagging and whistling your car down multiple times before you can even see the entrance of the park. Do not stop! Keep going until you can see the entrance, and there will be a well trusted parking area on the LEFT right before the entrance. They’re called Chalo Parking, and it costs 5,000 colones ($10USD) to park here. They also may need to take your key and do valet parking because it gets cramped in there. IT IS OKAY TO TRUST THEM. To ensure a parking spot at the closest parking area, get to the park between 7:00-7:30am.
One of the best Natural Park I have ever been in!! And the beach is amazing too

Way far for something you can see from anywhere else in Costa Rica, unless you are in a central town, not relaxing, tourist trap,  the mirador in Jaco has more wildlife for free, without getting your boxers size to allow entry

Must book tickets online or park entrance will not be allowed. The park it’s beautiful, as far as the beaches. There are many of them in different areas and the water is extremely good and nice. Crystal Clear at least now in May. The only downside is that before entering the park, way before the actual park entrance, there are lots of men in the street asking if you have a guide and trying to make you park where they are. They even get a little aggressive/intimidating. All you have to say is “yes I have a guide” and they will leave you alone. To park, we parked right outside the park where there are camaras, for 5k colones.

Also, to find the monkeys, you have to go all the way down and to the left.
Es necesario ir con guía para poder observar la distinta fauna
Excelente ubicación, comodidad y ubicación.
Amazing experience! Definitely worth the trip.
Lindo y controlan para mantener en perfecto estado

Such a beautiful park!
As we walked in we saw so much monkeys, lizards and crabs
I really recommend taking a guide because they can help you see a lot of animal (we saw snake, sloth and small birds and more that we wouldn't be able to see without a guide)
After all it was a unique experience!
Bonita área pero el mar es muy bravo
C'est un parc qui ne deceptionne jamais, la plage magnifique, toilettes propres et animaux  facile à observer. Seulement à éviter en saison très haute mais il y a de la place pour tout le monde
Hermoso y con mucha biodiversidad, playa linda. solo cuidado con los estafadores que están afuera vendiendo tours y mucho ojo con los monos, saben abrir mochilas.
Me encantó, solo planeen su tiempo por que la comida en el restaurante deja mucho que desear
¡Parque maravilloso con un bosque y unas playas preciosas! 😍

I absolutely LOVED this place and if you are a nature and animal lover you will too!

We got the earliest possible reservation and it was worth it as we had a few hours of peace and quiet before the crowds started to come in. However, they only let a certain amount of people in at a time to keep crowds under control. NOTE: You MUST make your time slot or risk not being let in.

Once in you literally step into a prestine jungle with stunning protected beaches. Wildlife is everywhere, but it is very hot and humid so on the day I went the animals weren't that active.

Make sure you know where to park and decide if you want a tour guide because as soon as you pull up you will have locals hustling you for both. They weren't rude or aggressive, you just have to be firm with them.

Be prepared to WALK and make sure to have plenty if water in a reusable container as they don't allow outside food or beverages (for beverages, specially no plastic bottles). The trailers aren't that bad, but there are a ton of stairs so take your time.

There are stunning vistas everywhere so the trailers and stairs are worth it. PRO TIP: Bring binoculars if you want to get the clearest views of animals. Also, if you really love nature and animals it might be worth a two day tip as the park closes at 3PM for entrance and everyone has to leave by 4PM local time. So, I'd suggest explore and enjoy the beaches and trails day one and scout of the best pictures and animal habitats. Then on day 2 bring all your equipment for those professionals shots where you can take your time and not be rushed.

Lastly, the beaches are awesome but the tide comes in quick so be sure to go early and enjoy the beaches first and hike afterwards if you planned to enjoy the beaches.

Pura vida!!
De los mejores parques nacionales qué hay, buenos senderos, lindos miradores y playa hermosa
Lots of wildlife

Manuel Antonio National Park es una auténtica pasada. Súper accesible y fácil de recorrer incluso. La combinación entre selva tropical y playas espectaculares hace que el paisaje tenga un contraste increíble.

Además, el parque está muy bien cuidado, es amigable para visitar y en unas horas puedes ver naturaleza, fauna y algunas de las playas más bonitas que he visto. Totalmente recomendado.

As other reviews mentioned, we got pulled over by guys in uniform pretending to be work workers. They had a stop point before you get to the beach. Tried to charge us $150 for two people, saying you can’t buy the park tickets for today, you have to buy a day in advance and you can park only here, to the right for the park and to the left for the beach for a fee. They were extremely rude. I ended up driving away. Then after passing the beach, several more guys were flagging us down, each trying to get us to park in their spots. I was in a bad mood before even getting to the park. They were threatening with police saying that if we don’t park where they say, the traffic police might fine us and take away our license plates. We ended up parking in the big parking lot right before the park entrance and paid 4000 colones ($9 US).

At the park entrance and throughout, we were getting harassed by the tour guides telling us to pay them and not go by ourselves.

(Be aware, you cannot bring plastic bottles into the park or any food, they will make you throw it away. Totallly ok, just something to be aware of. We had metal bottles so it was no problem.)

Inside the park, we did see some monkeys and crabs, on one of the paths upcoming tour guide with a group warned us there’s a viper snake on the side and not to go there as it is deadly if bites so we turned around. Grateful for the warning!

Park itself was ok, we were coming from La Fortuna and I’ve seen many national parks before, so this was underwhelming. Too many people (even on Monday), felt very commercialized.

I wouldn’t come here for the park, but the beach at the end was gorgeous! If anything, it’s worth coming here if you don’t mind dealing with swarms of scammers.

Viel zu viele Leute! Völlige Touristen-Abfertigung. Zuerst muss man Schlange stehen um das Gepäck durchchecken zu lassen. Danach läuft man auf einer breiten Strasse mit der Masse mit. Am Rande dieser Strasse befinden sich die Tiere. Jeder Guide erkämpft sich seinen Platz für seine Gruppe. Eine gestresste Zeit und ein gestresster Guide. Schade, eigentlich wäre der Guide sehr kompetent gewesen und er hat uns mit dem Natel schöne Foto ab dem Teleskop gemacht.
Im Restaurant kauften wir ein teures Sandwich das so trocken war wie die Sahara!
Ein Stern für den wirklich schönen Strand bei dem man auch Baden darf und ein Stern für unseren Rückweg auf einem Nebenweg, neben der Strasse wo wir auch noch schöne Mangroven sahen.
Amazing park with lots of animals. Easy to walk and great chance to go for a swim in the ocean.

פארק יפה ומרשים עם חופים יפים. תגיעו שבעים, אי אפשר להכניס אוכל והאוכל בפנים... תזמינו כרטיסים מראש, תביאו בגדי ים לחופים היפים. לא בטוח שממש תראו את החיות. לגבי הדרכה - תלוי בהעדפה שלכם, בעיני לא הכרחי.
Senderos limpios accesibles agua para abastecer

Recomendable guia.
Sino ni ves animalitos

Obiekt przygotowany organizacyjnie i logistycznie na przyjmowanie tysięcy turystów, rozbudowana i zadbana infrastutura, ale jeśli chodzi o faunę, to więcej zwierząt na Kostaryce spotkałem poza parkami i rezerwatami.
Amazing…. Hire a private guide, absolutely worth the money!
Très très cool et plages magnifiques :)
Bonito lugar , solo que los guías quieren venderte a fuerzas tus servicios

Importante si quieren hacer los senderos escoger el horario de la mañana ya que calienta 🥵 terrible al medio día.
Prohibido el ingreso de alimentos de ningún tipo ni frutas snacks nada, el Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio cuenta con cafetería, No se pueden ingresar botellas plásticas de un solo uso, Vapeadores ni bloqueadores o bronceadores en Aerosol, cuentan con casilleros por si ocupan dejar algo guardado.

Onze verwachtingen voor dit park waren niet hoog en deze werden ook bevestigd toen we door dit park liepen. Het minste park van onze hele vakantie en écht overrated. Het is gewoon geen mooi park, klein en heel druk. We hebben weliswaar veel dieren gezien, er zijn stranden met douches, maar voor ons geen aanrader.
We saw lots of wildlife, no need to get a guide, everything is well marked and fun to walk by yourself.
Beautiful park.  Lots of great animals and plants to see.  Don't touch the trees.  Some of them are poisonous.
Prix de 6$ nous avons payer environs  15$  à  nous 2 avec les tâches. Lieu magnifiques. Nous avons bien aimer

Tourist trap.
They try to get you to do the tour, and it is expensive. If you decline, they tell you you have to purchase tickets online for the next day. The guy tried making us pay for parking and the park ranger was confused as to why the guy was trying to make us pay. We left and wont be back.  I am disappointed in Costa Rica in general.

The beach about a mile down from the national park is beautiful, but like everything and everywhere, the people will charge you $20 to use the lawn chairs.

Parque Nacional con mucha fauna pero difícil de ver debido a la espesa vegetación.
Aconsejable coger primera hora. Si sudas mucho cógete una toalla pequeña. Lleva también agua y botas.
Increíble lugar para disfrutar naturaleza y descanso en la playa.

Un lugar que vale la pena llegar muy temprano para poder realizar todos los senderos y aprovechar las playas tan bellas que tiene.
100% recomendado y de los lugares obligados a visitar

Bonjour je vous conseille de prendre un guide pour voir de près certains animaux comme le paresseux très bien caché dans les arbres. Le parc est grand les points de vues sont magnifiques, les singes capucins sont très visibles partout. Les plages sont superbes. Si vous avez besoin d un guide j ai les coordonnées de celui avec qui j ai fait le tour

Beautiful park. Saw a lot of wildlife entering at 7:40am. Trails easy to walk. Amazing beaches. The lack of parking and the harassment outside by the touts is a horrible way to arrive. Worth a visit for sure but plan parking ahead of arrival
An amazing reserve with a variety of amazing landscapes and animals
Como todos los PN de Costa Rica entradas caras y circuitos reducidos. Muy pocos animales. Lo bueno son las playas donde quedarse hasta que cierra
Una meta imperdibile, abbiamo visto bradipi, iguane, scimmie, granchi colorati etc etc, inoltre è possibile fare il bagno in spiagge incantevoli 🇨🇷Superconsigliato!!!🇨🇷

Drive an hour an a half to see the park, we got there with 30 mins left and they wouldn't let us in.  No where on the website did we see that you need a reservation.  The town will try to sell you everything and anything, tourist trap

Es un lugar mágico
Una explicación muy divertida y estimulante

He visitado varios parques antes de este en Costa Rica y este no está sobrevalorado como lo puedan decir otras personas. Las playas son preciosas y hay muchos monos que puedes ver de cerca. Ojo fui en temporada “baja”. Pero realmente todos los turistas se quedan en el mismo punto y no se aventuran, así que nada más es escoger los demás senderos y te encuentras totalmente solo.

Mooi park, aapjes genoeg, luiaard ook. Wel minder vogels als in andere parken naar mijn idee.

Entree is waardeloos geregeld. Zorg ervoor als je zonder gids gaat je kaartjes al hebt gekocht.
Todo es hermoso!
Una experiencia hermosa, avistamiento de fauna diversas y playas turquesas
Très beau parc mais foule de monde! Ne pas hésiter à partir à la 2 e plage, aussi belle mais déserte!

Hermoso el parque y sus playas. Recomiendo hacerlo si o si con guía para ver bien de cerca a los animales e ir temprano. En nuestro caso, había bastante gente en las playas, así que nos quedamos hasta el mediodía y después nos fuimos a Espadilla
Ve con guía -> David fue excelente búsquenlo
Nunca funciona la web para comprar las entradas
Very beautiful park, easy to walk around and plenty of wildlife, we had a great time there, the beach is awesome 🌞🌴❤️
Me encantó el recorrido, pudimos avistar animales en su hábitat natural y disfrutar de una playa perfectas!! Maravillosa experiencia
Beautiful, lots of animals
Muy lindo, vimos varios animalitos pero creo que pudimos haber visto más si los grupos grandes de turistas no se la pasaran gritando. Fue una gran experiencia:)

A really wonderful national park, which houses sloths, a wide variety of monkeys, birds, insects, butterflies and much more. It is highly recommended to hire a guide if you want to spot the different animals there, because they hide very well. Use only an official or certified guide (they can be spotted by the telescope and the badge). Don't miss it!

El parque en si es un sueño, la naturaleza por si sola es un 5 estrellas. El puntaje de 3 estrellas se lo doy a las personas básicamente. En nuestra llegada 8am se nos tiraban encima para que estacionamos el auto (personas con identificación) y se lo dejamos a un muchacho que nos trató muy mal. Luego en la entrada te sacan comida y bebidas, la comida se entiende porque no hay que alimentar a los animales pero asi y todo adentro hay un restaurante entonces no se conduce con lo de la entrada, claramente es un negocio. Luego a diferencia de cahuita por ejemplo, hay mucha mas gente y tuvimos un guía de un grupo que andaba por ahí que palmeo dos veces a mi novia para hacerla apurar porque habia un perezoso y realmente fue muy maltratador. Todo esto que les cuento hace que mi puntaje no sea 5, la playa y los animales son y serán siempre un 1000 estrellas ♡
A small but fauna wise big park. Book a guide and enjoy the wildlife.
Magnifique parc qui regorge d'animaux typiques du Costa Rica. A visiter absolument !

Honestly there are other places to see wildlife than here! The park is overcrowded with tourists and you don't see much wildlife except insects and monkeys.

You can't bring plastic water bottles or ANY food! Eat before going because the food inside the park canteen is absolutely horrible. There is a line to go pay for the food, another line to pickup the food, another line to pick up the coffee! What a waste of time and after all this the food is bland and inedible!!

The beaches are beautiful but too rough to swim in plus there are jellyfish in the water. When I was there no one was swimming due to the storng currents that pull you in. There are more beautiful beaches in the world. I felt so disappointed by this trip overall.

Gorgeous and very flat / accessible. Definitely recommend a gauge so you see the animals. They are skilled at spotting them and showing you through the scope. Can also take photos for you that way. There is no way we would’ve seen the slots without the guide, showing us exactly where they were!
Que lugar maravilhoso. Passamos um dia espectacular, os animais sao impresionantes, as praias lindas e otimas para refrescar do calor.
Getting tickets was difficult. Totally worth it. Gorgeous beaches, sloths, monkeys, snakes.

Un parc superbe avec beaucoup d'environnements différents et énormément d'animaux à voir. Faire attention lors du choix du guide, la plupart se contentent de faire le sentier principal (celui des paresseux) qui est par conséquent très rempli...
We went with high expectations..

Beautiful diverse park, but crowded and somewhat overhyped. We entered at 7am and explored the park without a guide. We saw squirrel monkeys, howler monkeys, capuchin monkeys, agouti, coati, three fingered sloth, iguana, green poison dart frogs, great tinamou, crabs, and other bird and reptiles.

The park overall was an underwhelming experience for us. It's a huge park but the pathway we take has hardly anything to see. This may also be a function of our guide who hardly paid any attention to us and scooted quickly. We saw a sloth maybe hidden among the trees, and some birds and dragonflies - thats it. We don't really go into the vegetation.
The only positive here for us was the beaches. There were several beaches, and they were nice
Increíble parque rodeado de naturaleza playa y animales hermosos!

es pequeño pero se ven muchas cosas! recomiendo el tour guiado y si no… vas a ver cosas igual por que los grupos se paran a ver los animales. recomiendo el tour guiado para tener una mejor experiencia de todas maneras

Cool park and definitely amazing beaches with some that are more secluded for a more private lounge. However it is extremely crowded, and there are huge guided tour groups that block the walking passage, especially later on in the morning, so I would recommend to come in as early as possible. There is also little shade coverage so at around 12/1 pm it is straight sunshine- perfect for tanning but the UV is strong so can cause sunburn if you are not protected correctly. No food or plastic water bottles allowed. They have storage lockers but you have to pay for them ($5). Tickets need to be purchased in advance online.

Did a guided walking tour and thoroughly enjoyed my time at the park. At the end of the tour, you're dropped off at the beach and can enjoy a beach day. I recommend that you eat a good meal before the tour and bring water because you'll do a lot of walking, as the tour is about 2 hours. In addition, you've got to buy a ticket to the park through the government as most tours don't include your ticket to the park. You can risk buying "day of" but they may run out of passes for the hour you're visiting.

As you enter the park people will begin to ask you about parking, I recommend just saying you have a ticket and you have a parking lot close to the park so you don't have to walk long.
Très belle expérience ! Nous avons aimé marcher à travers la forêt / sentiers et voir la nature et les animaux.
De los mejores parques nacionales

Wow!!!! What an incredible experience.
We booked through Viator, entry plus guide was 39£ per person. During the two hour tour we saw a handful of different animals including two snakes, many lizards, iguanas, many birds, five different sloths, coatis, deers, bats, white faced monkeys. It was really amazing!!!!
Once the tour was finished, we walked around the park again two more times ( other guides kindly showed us through their telescopes the animals )
We then spent time on the beaches, paddling and cooling down in the water.
Overall you can easily spend from opening to
Close time wondering around and taking in the beautiful landscape and animals.
10/10 can’t recommend enough ⭐️
Beaux mais beaucoup trop de monde et les personnes ne respectent pas les animaux !
Beach was good.  I paid tour guides because they spot the animals.  They charge $80 per adult and it was not really worth it.
Must get a guide if you go. They have lockers and you can change into bathing suit for the two beaches.
Lindo parque aunque atestado de gente. La playa es de lo mejor que vi en costa rica (aunque no fui en plan de playas). Si o si entrar con guía sino no verás un solo animal.

It's a great national park for nature viewing and experiencing the jungle. However, it can get crowded, mostly because of tourists from overseas. No plastic bottles allowed, you need to carry your water in a flask. Inside the park you do have a cafeteria if you wish to have lunch but it can be pricey. There are multiple paths to take at this park but I recommend you go through them all, the park is open from 7am to 3pm so there's plenty of time.

Realmente el lugar es hermoso vale mucho la pena ir la playa es hermosa y  , ver la gran cantidad de animales que tenemos  es increíble
Si hay demasiada turismos extranjero y los precios al rededor son elevados para tenerlo en cuenta

Un sendero central y otros alternativos te llevan a conocer flora y fauna. Por momentos estas en el medio de la selva, que huele a tierra mojada y se oye el mar. Los monos andan por todos lados, es importante no alimentarlos y tampoco asustarlos. Solo dejar que ellos caminen con vos o anden libres por su hábitat.
Hay cangrejos, arañas, víboras, iguanas. Y hay tambien perezosos!
Al final del recorrido esta la playa para relajar y descansar. Ojo con las pertenencias porque los monos andan por ahi y querrán ver que tienes! Son curiosos.
Se puede ir de ojotas, pero recomiendo un calzado cómodo como zapatillas. Agua solo en botellas de metal. No se permite ingresar con comida o botellas de plástico.

Visitamos el Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio y, aunque es un lugar muy conocido, nuestra experiencia no fue del todo positiva.

Es importante saber que las entradas deben comprarse con bastante antelación, especialmente en época de vacaciones. De lo contrario, prácticamente la única opción es contratar un tour con agencia, lo que encarece bastante la visita.

Al llegar a la entrada, llama la atención la cantidad de negocio alrededor del parque: personas en la calle indicando (e insistiendo) dónde aparcar, ya que todos los aparcamientos son de pago. Esto genera una sensación algo incómoda desde el principio.

Dentro del parque, aunque el entorno es bonito, no tuvimos la suerte de ver muchos animales. Además, parte del recorrido transcurre por caminos de grava que no transmiten la sensación de estar en un entorno tan natural como en otros parques del país.

En conjunto, la experiencia tuvo más aspectos negativos que positivos. Una pena, porque esperábamos mucho más de este parque.
Lindo, completo y espectacular

Manuel Antonio National Park is a great place to visit. It can get a bit too crowded, but that’s because it’s such a beautiful and popular park.

It has a perfect balance of being wild yet very easy to walk around, with well-maintained paths. The beaches inside the park are stunning, and there’s plenty of wildlife—you can see a lot of animals along the way. I explored it on my own without a guide and still saw a lot of animals, which was amazing.

Overall, a really great park and definitely worth visiting.

Le parc est magnifique on y croise beaucoup d'espèces mais avec l'aide d'un guide. Prévoyez à l'avance avec des sociétés comme World évasion.

⚠️Attention lorsque que vous vous rendez au parc
⚠️Attention arnaque parking - Parc Manuel Antonio

À quelques centaines de mètres avant l'entrée du parc, des personnes en gilet vous font signe de vous arrêter en disant que le parking est complet.
🚨Ce ne sont PAS des employés officiels.
Leur but est de vous faire vous garer plus loin et vous faire payer plus cher.
Le vrai parking est juste à côté de l'entrée et coûte environ 5000 colones.
Conseil : ne vous arrêtez pas et continuez votre route.
Si on insiste, dites simplement que vous allez dans un hôtel à côté de l'entrée (comme l’hôtel Espadilla ou l’hôtel Villa Bosque), ils vous laisseront passer.
Wundervoller Park. Schöner Rundweg. Viel Bauernfängerei vor Eingang deshalb Tickets online kaufen (in CR generell). Viele Besucher verstehen nicht, dass man leise sein muss um Tiere zu sehen.
If you are on a short visit to Costa Rica this park is well worth visiting. You will be almost guaranteed of seeing a good cross section of wild life eg monies, sloths etc as well as a lovely beach.
Absolut overrated. Zuviele Touristen, wenig Tiere. Kein authentisches Gefühl. Wir haben viel mehr Tiere in Naturreservaten gesehen, mit 1:1 Verhältnis als hier. Ich würde nicht hingehen.
Incredible park and beach.

Un parc qui n’en vaut pas du tout la peine . Très peu d’animaux … j’en ai vu 10 fois plus à l’hôtel que dans le parc …
Parking : 10€ supplémentaire (en espèce uniquement) … les touristes sont vraiment considérés comme des vaches à traire.
Beautiful place and very easy to hike.

Unfortunately this beautiful place has been ruined by the people that work there.  Online ticket purchase takes so much of your personal information - email, home address, phone number, AND the passport numbers of each guest.  And of course your credit card.

From the moment you approach you'll be aggressively flagged by people jumping in front of your car trying to get you to park in their "secure" parking spot for a fee.

Walking through the intense security you are then harassed by "tour guides" that call you stupid for not wanting them and warn you that you're not going to see anything and you'll regret it.

Once inside the park, don't dare to look in the same direction as a guides telescope because they will again harass you for being cheap and not paying, ridiculing you in front of their group saying things like "see how these people follow us and don't pay".  We were walking on the same path... it's a park.  And they were looking at just an iguana... it's an iguana guy.

It's really a shame, if the people weren't so aggressively annoying I would definitely recommend this beautiful place.  If you're looking for calm nature, stay away.

Amazing coastal park with mix of rainforest trails and stunning beaches. Wildlife sightings like monkeys, sloths, and iguanas are common.

On the downside, it gets very crowded, especially midday, and entry requires planning with tickets and parking. Still, worth it
Amazing place full of nature, chance to see sloths, squirrel monkeys and other wildlife

Netter Park mit zwei wunderschönen Buchten, wo man gut schwimmen kann. Man kann den Park auf eigene Faust erkunden oder einen Guide mieten.
Den Hype, der um den Park gemacht wird, verstehe ich allerdings nicht.

Estuve en el parque la primera vez en el 2012, existía la entrada al parque nada más, hermoso lugar fauna increíble, muy lindo. Volví en 2022 la entrada al parque parece una ciudad. Ruidos, lleno de negocios, personas etc. El parque sufrió mucho. Casi no se ven cangrejos cuando 1o años antes era enorme la cantidad de ellos en los senderos. Muy pocos animales, principalmente turistas ruidosos. Lamentable.

En fait d'animaux, nous avons surtout vu des nuées de touristes...
Très déçus par ce parc où on se croirait à Disney Land, avec la foule et le bruit en permanence.
Le guide, très sympa et qualifié a fait de son mieux, mais ça reste le seul parc où, à défaut d'oiseaux, excepté le superbe mot mot, nous  avons vu beaucoup de pigeons comme nous.
Allez, on y a juste laissé quelques plumes, pura vida quand même...
When entering park the tour guides are very aggressive trying to get you to pay for tour.  It's way better enjoying the park & beaches at your own pace.

Assez mitigé sur ce parc.. Nous avons vu que quelques animaux en arrivant à l'ouverture et avec un guide. À partir de 10h, on ne voit plus d'animaux et il y a beaucoup de monde dans le parc. Si on fait le rapport prix/expérience, ce n'est pas le meilleur parc que nous ayons fait. Le point positif c'était la plage Manuel Antonio qui etait magnifique.
Super parc ; privilégier une visite guidée pour voir des animaux
Manuel Antonio National Park is home to some of the most stunning beaches in Costa Rica. At the end of the trail, you’re rewarded with breathtaking coastal views and pristine shores.

La nature est présente et vous allez voir des animaux…. Mais…. C’est tellement touristique que la vue d’un animal génère un embouteillage de guides et de touristes .
Si l’ambiance zoo te convient, go… sinon tu seras déçu
Great place in general saw 3 sloths tons of monkeys and many more species the main beach is very crowded but the other beaches are great overall must do in costa rica!!!

El 6 de abril visitamos el Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio con un grupo de 30 personas.

Lo primero que debo decir es que, cuando viajas con un grupo grande, la compra de entradas se vuelve bastante complicada. Sería mucho más práctico contar con opciones específicas para grupos, no necesariamente en taquilla, pero sí algún sistema que facilite este proceso.

En nuestro caso, llegamos aproximadamente 2 horas después de nuestra hora de entrada, y curiosamente esto jugó a nuestro favor, ya que había menos gente en los senderos. Gracias a eso pudimos disfrutar mucho más la experiencia y observar varias especies: monos capuchinos, una familia de monos tití a muy corta distancia, dos perezosos, serpientes, basiliscos, entre otros.

La única desventaja fue que ya no tuvimos mucho tiempo para disfrutar la playa, ya que el parque cierra relativamente temprano. Sin embargo, también es importante entender que estamos en un área protegida: nosotros somos los visitantes y es fundamental respetar los tiempos y el entorno de los animales.
En cuanto a las instalaciones, los baños actualmente están en malas condiciones, aunque se nota que están en proceso de mejora, lo cual es una excelente noticia.
Sobre los alimentos, hay un cambio muy positivo. Hace un año se permitía el ingreso con comida y eso generaba mucho desorden, sobre todo por la interacción con los monos. En esta ocasión, se percibe un mejor control, menos contacto con la fauna y un parque mucho más limpio.

Sin duda, es un lugar al que volveríamos. Solo dejaría como recomendación mejorar la agilidad en el proceso de acceso para grupos.
Maravilloso sitio, bien conservado, cuidado, ahí el turista se educa porque se educa. La fauna increíble y única. La playa es hermosa; el agua tibia y muy limpia. En general una gran experiencia.
Beautiful park.

Un lugar simplemente impresionante. El Parque Nacional Corcovado es de los pocos sitios donde la naturaleza se siente realmente intacta y salvaje. La selva es densa, llena de vida y cada sendero parece una pequeña expedición. Si te gusta la naturaleza en su estado más puro, este parque es una experiencia inolvidable. No es el lugar más fácil de visitar, pero precisamente eso es parte de su magia.
Great beach 5/5, the rest leaves much to be desired
Inolvidable
A definição é ligar incrível.

Cuidado con las personas que te detienen antes de llegar al parque y te venden los tours super caros.

Recomiendo ir con binoculares o contratar un guía para que puedas apreciar todo, sino es muy difícil ver los animales ya que la mayoría están lejos y solo se ven con los binoculares. También lo bueno del guia es que ya saben dónde están y te explican todos con detalles.

Lo bueno::: aunque fuimos sin guiar, vimos muuuuchos animales (serpiente, aves, monos, lagartos, venado, guatusa😍). Las playas son preciosas y la naturaleza también!

Lo malo::: en el parque grupos grandes de mucha gente que genera mucho barullo... No es un lugar tranquilo para estar en conexión con la naturaleza. Si no llevas botella metálica de agua propia, te venden dentro agua carísima y comida tampoco barata...

y lo peor::: que según estás llegando, por la carretera con el coche te para gente con chaleco fingiendo que es del parque, te piden mirar las entradas y tu pasaporte y de pronto te venden un tour😱 y te meten miedo con cosas como "¿quieres ir sólo a pasear o contratar un guía para ver animales?" Y si no estás bien informado o no entiendes el idioma, acaban engañandote...
Lots of wildlife to see. Best to go early to avoid the crowds and book a guide, as they spot things you won't.
Un parque gigante con muchos animales y las playas súper limpias
I know this park gets a lot of hype, but I think due to the touristy nature of it it’s become less magical. Still definitely worth visiting, even without a guide we saw a few animals!

This was one of the highlights of your trip. We saw capuchins, howlers, titis, iguanas, basilisks, all sorts of frogs, macaws, parrots, toucans. Additionally, there are several world class beaches in the park as nice as anything you have ever seen in a travel add.
Book your tickets at least a day in advance and chose an early slot. You have a better shot at seeing wildlife before the heat of the middle of the day.
You cannot bring food in on account of the animals but they have an eatery onsite.

Muy bonito parque, recomendacion de llevar tu botella de agua vacía y adentro la llenas, hay diversos bebederos de agua limpia. Lleva ropa cómoda, hacen mucho calor. Cuidado con los monos, a veces se llevan tus cosas. Lleva traje de baño para estar ahí en la playa.
Saw all sorts of animals and got to swim at a no crowded beach.

Definitely take a guided tour, with their help we could spot and observe a lot (sloths, monkeys, birds etc.). Ours (Ashley) was top. It was the smallest park we visited compared to others in La Fortuna and Monteverde, but the one with the most dense fauna. Main trail with the guide will take you no more than 1-1.5 hr, then take your time to visit the1- secondary trails and the beaches for swimming, in total approx. 4 hrs. Park closes at 4 PM
A great day, highly recommend getting a guide. I would have walked past all the animals without their excellent help.
Uno de los mejores parques nacionales de Costa Rica playa y bosques juntos

Visiting Manuel Antonio National Park is truly an unforgettable experience. This park offers a perfect combination of lush rainforest, abundant wildlife, and some of the most beautiful beaches in Costa Rica.
From the moment you enter, you are surrounded by nature. It is common to see monkeys, sloths, iguanas, and a variety of birds along the trails, so it is worth taking your time and looking closely. The walking paths are well maintained and accessible, making it enjoyable for visitors of all ages.
The beaches inside the park are absolutely stunning, with soft white sand and clear, calm water that is perfect for swimming. The contrast between the dense green jungle and the bright blue ocean creates breathtaking views.
Be prepared for crowds, especially during peak hours, and it is highly recommended to arrive early. Also, bring water, sunscreen, and patience, as the experience is worth it.
Overall, Manuel Antonio National Park is a must-visit destination for anyone traveling to Costa Rica. It offers a unique blend of adventure, relaxation, and natural beauty that leaves a lasting impression.
De mis favoritos

Wir haben den Nationalpark in der absoluten Hochsaison besucht – an unseren Besuchstag gab es keine Karten mehr zu kaufen!
Unsere Tipps für den Besuch:
1.) Am wichtigsten ist es sehr früh zu kommen. Am besten ist es schon kurz vor sieben am Eingang zu sein, da der Park bereit um 7:00 Uhr öffnet. die Tickets muss man vorab im Internet kaufen.
2.) Am besten mal kommt mit dem Bus oder Taxi, da der Parkplatz bereits um 7:30 Uhr komplette überfüllt ist und zudem 10$ Parkgebühr kostet. Das Taxi kostete von unserer Unterkunft nur fünf Dollar.
3.) Man braucht nicht unbedingt einen Guide für den Park, denn die meisten Tiere kann man auch selbst beobachten beziehungsweise wenn irgendwo ein Tier sichtbar ist bildet sich sowieso eine Traube von Menschen. Humor muss sich einfach nur dazu stellen, um das Tier zu beobachten.
4.) Der Park ist ziemlich klein und daher bilden sich schon in der Früh nach dem Eingangsbereich große Menschenansammlungen. Wir sind daher relativ schnell Richtung Punta Catedral gegangen und waren sowohl am Playa als auch auf dem Rundweg am Punta Catedral so gut wie alleine!
Zusammenfassend lässt sich sagen, dass der Nationalpark Sehr klein ist, jedoch bei frühen Besuch durchaus individuell begangen werden kann. Es gibt sehr viele Tiere in dem Nationalpark, die man am besten in der Früh sehen kann. Sobald sich der Park mit Menschen füllt, beziehungsweise es zur Mittagszeit heißer wird, ziehen sich die meisten Tiere ins Innere in Schattenplätze zurück!

A mi parecer muy sobre valorado,te lo venden como si hubiera demasiada fauna pero realmente no hay mucho que ver, vimos mas perezosos y monos en nuestro ABNB. El precio de los guías es excesivo y realmente no se necesita,  se ve lo mismo con o sin guia.
Great place to visit. Lots of monkeys and sloths. Beach is great
Wunderschön, aber wie vielfach zu lesen, ab dem späteren Vormittag sehr voll. Wer dem entgehen will, sollte allerspätestens 7:30 Uhr am Eingang sein, der Park öffnet um 7 Uhr.
Scenic calm beaches, interesting hiking and we needed a guide to find all the amazing animals hiding under our noses

Ich war vor 11 Jahren das erste mal im Nationalpark und war begeistern. Diesmal war ich enttäuscht.

Für den Nationalpark muss man vorab ein Ticket online kaufen. Wir haben 5 Tage im voraus buchen müssen auf SINAC, weil alles andere ausverkauft war. Ein Ticket kostet 16 Dollar.

Wir sind mit dem Auto hingefahren und wollten ohne Guide entspannt durch den Park laufen.

Vor dem Park ist ein Parkplatz bei dem man 5000 Colones oder 10 Dollar Cash zahlt. Auf dem Weg dort hin wurden wir angehalten und uns wurde gesagt, dass wir ohne Guide nur zum Strand können und nicht den Park und Tiere sehen dürften. Ich hatte vorher bereits gelesen, dass so einige Guides versuchen Besucher zu einem Guide zu überreden.

Plastik Trinkflaschen und Essen sind aus Naturschutzgründen verboten. Man kann feste Wasserflaschen mitnehmen. In der Mitte des Parks gibt’s ein Restaurant. Das Essen sah schlecht aus und Getränke sind teuer. Unverständlich war, warum man Cola in einem Pappbecher bekommt aber Wasser nur in einer festen Flasche für 9 Dollar. Die Flasche ist schön gemacht und kann ein Souvenir sein. Wir wollten nur Wasser trinken, haben es aber nicht eingesehen 9 Dollar zu zahlen.

Der Park war überfüllt mit Menschen. Wir haben in der freien Natur Costa Ricas und anderen Parks bereits fast alle Tiere gesehen, die wir auch dort gesehen haben. Die Atmosphäre an anderen Orten war viel schöner.

Der Strand hat uns an einige Strände auf Mallorca erinnert. Wer die Insel kennt, weiß was ich meine. Wir sind hier direkt wieder weg, nachdem wir Tiere im Park gesehen haben und sind zu einem unberührten Strand an der Pazifikküste gefahren (ohne Eintrittsgebühren).
Der Strand ist bestimmt ganz toll, wenn nicht so viele Menschen da wären.

Ich würde hier nicht nochmal hingehen.

Tour guide was very knowledgeable. First time visiting park and saw so much. Learned alot about the natural inhabitants. Guide helped take really good pictures.  The beach at the end of the tour was fabulous.

Sin duda, es uno de los parques más hermosos de Costa Rica. La playa está impecable, el entorno es espectacular y se nota el gran esfuerzo por proteger y conservar la fauna que habita en el lugar.
El único aspecto a mejorar es el tema de los guías. Resulta curioso que, en un país tan ordenado, esta parte parezca poco regulada: desde personas que interceptan a los visitantes antes de llegar al estacionamiento, hasta el hecho de que, sin un guía, puede ser difícil apreciar bien a los animales, aunque estén a la vista de todos.
Mi recomendación sería establecer una regulación más clara, integrando el servicio de guías de forma oficial, con tarifas definidas y una oferta transparente que mejore la experiencia para todos los visitantes.

La visita a este Parque Nacional, fue una experiencia agradable, con senderos muy bien cuidados, en donde encontramos gran variedad de aves, oso hormiguero, perezoso, y como hay manglares hay cocodrilos, una playa muy linda, pero hay que caminar bastante, no permiten ingresar alimentos, pero hay dentro para comer, en donde las jaulas son para los visitantes evitando que los monos traviesos no te quiten tus alimento, muy vigilado.

Do tre stelle principalmente per il mare, che è davvero spettacolare e da solo vale la visita. Anche la vegetazione è molto bella, rigogliosa e ben conservata, ed è piacevole passeggiare nei sentieri immersi nella natura.
Purtroppo però l’esperienza complessiva non è all’altezza delle aspettative. La presenza di animali è stata quasi nulla, vedere un pappagallo o un tucano è praticamente impossibile, onestamente mi aspettavo molto di più da un parco di questo livello... visto come viene presentato..
Altro punto critico è l’organizzazione, già dall’ingresso si percepisce confusione e poca gestione dei flussi, con attese e poca chiarezza.
In sintesi, posto molto bello dal punto di vista paesaggistico, ma con una gestione migliorabile e un’esperienza naturalistica decisamente sotto le aspettative.

Falta mas animales para ver. Y los tours guiados son en exceso cortos, se terminan rapido las entradas por lo cual uno debe comprar indefectiblemente con guía y en ocasiones disminuye el tiempo de permanencia dentro del parque

Ein recht preiswerter (ca. 5-6€) Nationalpark, welcher aber massiv überlaufen ist. Wir haben in etwa 100m Reichweite auf einem sehe großen Parkplatz geparkt und 5000 colones bezahlt.
Den Park haben wir mit einem Guide gebucht, welcher circa 50€ (2 stunden) gekostet hat.
Ohne den Guide wären uns diverse Tiere nicht aufgefallen, was also sehr hilfreich und informativ war.

Die erste Hälfte des Nationalparks ist relativ kurz, danach kommt ein wunderschöner Strand zum Entspannen. Wir haben während unserer Costa Eica Reise einige Strände besucht und sind der festen Überzeugung, dass der Strand im Nationalpark der schönste ist!

Die Mitnahme von Getränken in Plastikflaschen, sowie die Mitnahme von Speisen sind ausdrücklich verboten. Eine wiederbefüllbare Flasche aus Metall beispielsweise ist allerdings erlaubt.
love the park but so difficult to buy a ticket online without also paying for a guide, wish the website was easier when purchasing tickets

Increíble!!!!! Maravilloso!!!
Debes sacar turno con antelación, generalmente con dos días de anticipación a la fecha que quieres ir.
Concurre al parque sin alimentos, ni bebidas. Hay bebederos, duchas, baños, y un bar al cual no recomiendo ir, había mal olor en el ingreso, y no era buena la atención  ni el  menú

Great park, we went whitout a guide and still managed to see different kinds of monkeys, frogs and other wildlife.

There is also cool beaches you can visit inside the park for a swim to cool down.

Overall a great experience, you are not allowed to bring single use plastic so I recommend bringing a water bottle as the prices inside are wild. 10$ for 750ml of water.

I am giving a one star to this place for the management, since we booked online as advised, since park entrance only was sold out we booked a guided tour which included parking, a guide, and a parking , 4 of us coasted us ~$400. Actual cost to park is ~$20, plus cost of tickets would be ~$80, which would be $100 for all us. The guide really didn’t care about the tour and just rushed to get to the beach as quickly as possible. Overcrowded with lots of tourists trying to the same thing, you really can’t enjoy the nature because everyone is there looking at the same one wild life. You don’t benefit a guide to walk ~2km, very well marked. If you show up on the day of and get the individual ticket and just decline every guild that approaches you and go straight to the beach.
Espectacular lugar. Una experiencia maravillosa. Recomendado!

Muy recomendable. Accesos buenos. En el interior hay baños, duchas y zonas para cambiarse. La rutas están señalizadas. No te dejan acceder con comida para que no te atquen los animalitos. Recomiendo hacer el tour con un guía para poder ver más animales que pueden pasar desapercibidos a simple vista. Ojo con dejar las mochilas en la playa, los monos a veces intentan abrirlas.
Must see for wildlife

Hermosa experiencia, ir lo más temprano posible, ya que la playa es más ancha y permite disfrutar mejor!
A la tarde con sube el mar y casi no tenés plata!!
Cuidado con los monos!!! Abren carteras, mochilas buscando comida!! No sé confíen en dejar cerca de los árboles sus pertenencias!!
Parque muito bonito
Tolle Führung & Tiere zu sehen, am Ende ein bisschen Strand. Schöner Tag!

Jättevacker och trevlig park att gå runt i några timmar. Det är väldigt rikt djurliv, vi såg massor av olika djur såsom apor och sengångare! Man kan dessutom bada vid någon av stränderna samt det finns fina utkiksplatser. Du ser väldigt mycket utan en guide men en guide kan vara bra om du vill se ännu mer, på nära håll och få information om djuren.

Wir sind um 7:30 gestartet, nur kurze Schlsnge an der Taschen- und Ticketkontrolle. Cholo Parking direkt am Eingang. Nicht auf die rüden Parktanweiser mit Trillerpfeife reagieren. Relativ viele Tiere gesehen. Sehr schöne Bademöglichkeit am Ende unserer Führung. Ohne Guide hätten wir sehr wenig gesehen.

Belle reserve naturelle. Ne vous laissez pas scamer par les guides en amont du parc qui vous disent que vous allez rien voir et que vous ne pouvez que voir la plage... c'est faux... certes vous allez voir plus avec un guide et ses jumelles mais vous pouvez accéder partout sans eux...
Muy bonito lugar playas excelentes, pero demasiadas personas

Increíble el parque y con las visitas guiadas Nocturna con el guia Harold y la diurna con la guía Karla nos han presentado dos maneras muy distintas de apreciar sus gran atractivos: la fauna y la flora!!

Très belle expérience, nous sommes venus dès l’ouverture (7h) et avons pu profiter du calme avant les grosses vagues de touristes. Nous avons eu les plages pour nous tous seuls quasiment. Également vu beaucoup de singes sur la boucle au croisement des plages Espadilla et Manuel Antonio. On a profité de la plage Manuel Antonio pendant quelques heures. Après nous avons fait un crochet par le mirador (attention bcp de marches à la montée)
Dernier détour par la cascade proche de l’entrée, mais malheureusement à cette période de l’année il n’y a presque pas d’eau, donc sentier pour rien
Pour le parking, on recommande El Chalo (10$ pour la journée) juste à côté de l’entrée du parc. Ne vous faites pas avoir par le gros parking juste après le virage.

I visited MA National Park in April 2026 & this park is must to do activities in Manual Antonio.

Park was crowded  for early morning season. This is absolutely fun park with lots of Animals, specially monkeys, Sloths, birds, snakes, lizards & frogs.
Young kids will have lots of fun.

Within the park, there multiple beaches & they were beautiful & clean water.
There are showers, restrooms & a food restaurant inside the park.

Don't bring plastic water bottles with you, park doesn't allow you to carry inside.
Also keep rain jacket/umbrella with you along with swimwear & towels. You won't be able to buy any swimwear inside the park, however there are many shops just outside the entrance.
Espectacular... Entorno bellísimo.

Beautiful park. The reservation system is poorly implemented. They allow travel agencies to prebook tickets. This severely limits availability for the public. The tour agencies charge twice for the tickets and you must also purchase a tour from them.

The system is being abused.
Très bien

Beautiful hike with lots of monkeys and beautiful beaches. Don't bring plastic bottles!!! You'll have to toss them at the entrance. They said there were fill stations but we only found one 3/4 of the way back. We had long been out of water since then.
This company and his owner has a bad customer service. He told me that he don’t want to talk to me if I don’t have cash to pay him.

This is a must if you are planning to be in the area. You can do the trails self guided but if you are really wanting to see the wildlife and learn more about the ecosystem you will want to book a tour guide. This is a very busy park so book well in advance. A point to note: you are not allowed to bring any kind of food into the park. There is a cafeteria that is located by the beach. Be prepared...The prices are very high (which is to be expected) and unfortunately the quality of food is really poor. Stick to the fruit cups and smoothies if you can. The sandwiches are not worth it. The beach is beautiful too!

Nos encantó el parque de Manuel Antonio. Vimos un montón de animales gracias a nuestra guía Beatriz que fue lo mejor de todo que además de ser muy simpática se notaba su pasión por su trabajo, su gran  conocimiento así como su gran habilidad para captar los animales y estar pendiente de nosotros.
La mejor guía que nos pudo haber tocado!
Excelente experiencia. Una maravilla y vale mucho hacerla con guía

Esta muy masificado de turistas, después de haber visitado otros parques nacionales este me ha parecido el peor, no merece la pena pagar el dineral que se paga con guía. Recomiendo sacar la entrada por libre por visitarlo
Disney-esque.  Huge crowds, pushy guides, few animals or birds.  Stupidly overpriced.  Avoid.
Es un parque muy lindo, la playa también, solo tener cuidado con los hermosos monitos que les gusta abrir las mochilas y llevarse las cosas jajaja

Muita gente, muita exploração e pouca vida animal.
Atenção: os guias autorizados estão DENTRO do parque. No estacionamento fui abordado por um suposto guia oficial que me confirmou ser guia oficial (farda e cartão). Paguei 60 dólares ppr duas pessoas mas percebi que não era ele o guia que ia connosco. Confrontei-o e disse que queria tirar uma foto do cartão de identificação de guia oficial, mas recusou.
Prdi-lhe de imediato a devolução do dinheiro!
Wildlife everywhere! Highly recommend you use a guide since the wildlife is hard to spot on your own.  Beaches are beautiful swimmable beaches.

Manuel Antonio es una reserva natural en la que puedes admirar distintas especies de animales, yo contrate Tour para ir con guía pero siento que no vale mucho la pena. El guía te va mostrando las especies que van encontrando y después te ofrecen un snack, y después te dejan por tu cuenta ir a la playa. Pero sin problema puedes tu ir por tu cuenta, eso sí, debes comprar con tiempo tu ticket de entrada y si quisieras guía ahí puedes contratar uno. Las playas son bonitas y calidas.
Too busy, but some good wildlife.
Es hermoso. Miradores al mar preciosos y gran variedad de animalitos en su hábitat natural
Excellent and beautiful place.
Una experiencia diferente, muy recomendable para los que les gusta la naturaleza.

Amazing experience at Manuel Antonio with our kids! We saw sloths, monkeys, frogs, bats, and so much wildlife super exciting for the whole family. The trails are easy, the scenery is beautiful, and the beach is absolutely stunning. Definitely a must visit! 🌿🐒🌊
We had an amazing day with Beatriz. Despite the downpour, she made sure we had a good time and was incredibly knowledgeable and fun. Can only recommend.

You can see quite some animals if you are lucky, but that’s the only nice part about this park. It’s become way too commercial which you notice as soon as you enter the park. It is very very crowded and it’s hard to even walk freely. The number of people they allow ruins your experience. There are more attractive and quiet national parks to visit in Costa Rica.
Beautiful park but wayyyyy to many people allowed in at once

EDIT: this place is a tourist trap. From the unofficial parking officers to the loud and obnoxious tourists and guides that likely scared all the animals deeper into the forest. Avoid, save your money and find somewhere else to go. I saw more animals just driving around CR

El parque es hermoso !! lindas playas ,  muchos animales para ver , los caminos muy bien cuidados y señalizados pero lamentablemente muy masivo demasiada gente ojala puedan reveer el tema de las entradas que se dan por dia.

Lots of monkeys, sloths but rather hard to find, great beach, paths in good condition and well signposted. Quite busy. Rather long queue to enter even with tickets bought in advance. Well worth a day wandering around.

Super parc, nous n’avions pas pris de guide et n’avons pas regretté. Nous avons vu beaucoup de singes, des gros crabes, des iguanes, et même une biche !! Il faut y aller le plus tôt possible c’est vraiment très touristique et ça ferme tôt (15h00).
Espectacular 👌!! Pura vida!!!
Great place to take in Costa Rican wildlife.

I don't really get why it's so popular, maybe we just got unlucky and there weren't that many animals, but beyond some far off sloths there wasn't really much to see. Sort of a nice walk through the trees on a large but extremely busy trail, huge your groups everywhere crowding around what little there is to see. The ticket system is super annoying have to book far in advance for a date and time, and definitely sells out, I think mostly to guides trying to sell overpriced tours. Beach area is beautiful, but so is the public beach that you don't need a ticket to go to. Unless we were just unlucky or there at the totally wrong time of year (early April) I have no idea what the big draw was. If you want a pleasant walk perhaps the regular ticket price worth it, I would definitely skip any expensive guides. Word of warning the check your bags at the entrance and you can not bring in disposable plastic water bottles.

Ein sehr schöner Nationalpark am Meer schöner Sandstrand der zum Relaxen einlädt.Ein Spaziergang bis zur Walflosse ist sehr schön.
Wichtig ist das man auf jedenfall auf die Ebbe und Flut achtet,sonst wird es kritisch.Das rauchen im Nationalpark ist strengstens verboten.Der Eintritt kostet 6 Dollar plus 10% Service.
Waste of money and time. It’s boring and your tour guide shows you crab, hummingbird, butterflies, or lizards, and maybe a slot. It’s not worth it.

Breath-taking!
If you wanna enjoy the beaches, arrive early as about 10 am it gets super busy!

Manuel Antonio National Park is often praised as one of Costa Rica’s most beautiful destinations, and the scenery truly lives up to the reputation. Unfortunately, visiting during high season comes with a major downside: overwhelming crowds. We arrived early in the morning, yet the park was already packed with large groups and countless guides leading small tours along the main trails. It felt congested from the very beginning, which made it hard to enjoy the experience fully.

The beach, usually a highlight of the park, was also completely overcrowded after the tours ended. Only on a few of the smaller side trails—those not used by the guides—did we find some peace and quiet. While the natural beauty is undeniable, the sheer number of visitors significantly limited the overall experience.
Pristine Beaches and amazing wild life.

Joli parc a faire si vous passez dans le coin.

Prendre un guide est sympa pour voir plus d'animaux. Si vous vous balades dans les endroits moins convoité des touristes vous pourrez aussi voir d'autres animaux par vous même.

Pas possible de prendre de nourriture dans le parc. Il y a une cafétéria avec des choses à manger. C'est loin d'être de la grande cuisine mais ça fonctionne pour manger sur le pouce
Extraordinaria experiencia, el lugar muy bonito y bastante bien administrado, el recorrido y las playas muy padres, totalmente recomendado.
Maravilloso todo!

The park it self is extremely beautiful and a must visit !!

It is warm/hot so bring a non disposable form of water like a yeti or something like that

Depending on your guide or whether you go alone can make a big difference, since the guides know what to look for in the trees

The guides do seemed rushed to get as many groups as they can so that can be sucky

If you don’t jump in the ocean (which the guides told us can be dangerous  because of the waves and riptides l) the trail could be walked in about 2 hours

Do not try to bring in any of the things that are posted not to like sunscreen snacks tobacco, they do check your bags and will make you throw it away or put it in a locker

Tons of sloths, monkeys, birds to be seen  !

When getting to the park beware of people trying to get you to park in their lots ! Some seemed sketchy and a lot of the people seemed quite pushy fighting over cars.

But that’s on the outside of the park

Kid friendly

Wheelchair friendly

One time kind of thing

Nice park and beach inside.
Be aware that food and drinks are not allowed from outside and the prices inside are high.
Gum is considered food, so leave it in the car or will be trashed

Nationalpark Manuel Antonio – absolute Enttäuschung.

Komplett überlaufen, man wird durchgeschleust wie in einem Freizeitpark. Von Naturerlebnis keine Spur. Kaum Tiere gesehen – und wenn, dann nur mit Glück und zwischen Menschenmassen.

Die Organisation ist schlecht, das Ticketsystem unnötig kompliziert und mühsam. Vor Ort fühlt sich niemand wirklich zuständig, die Mitarbeitenden wirken desinteressiert und unmotiviert.

Preis-Leistung stimmt überhaupt nicht. Viel zu teuer für das, was man bekommt.

Fazit: Spart euch den Aufwand. Einer der enttäuschendsten Orte in Costa Rica.

Massively overcrowded park, over touristed and very few animals to see - which is much different than it was the last time we visited 8 years ago. If you’re traveling from San Jose, be warned that the road between San Jose and Manuel Antonio is under major construction. Expect at least 6 hours, as opposed to 3 hours normal travel time. Hours of sitting in the sun, waiting for construction. Air conditioning off to prevent car overheating. It was not worth it, a trip spoiler.
Beautiful, nature at its best.

Nous avons fait la visite avec le guide Danny Garcia vraiment génial il est passionné et a vraiment l’œil pour trouver les animaux les plus camouflés je vous recommande vivement de prendre ce guide car sinon vous ne verrez rien prenez un guide juste à l’entrée du parc avec une chemise officielle car il peut y avoir des arnaques il faut payer 20 dollar en cash par personne c’est beaucoup moins cher que les prix proposés par les hôtels

Manuel Antonio is a must for anybody that is visiting Costa Rica for wildlife viewing. It is good to take a guided tour, because many of the guides share wildlife spottings with each other.
Also, if you don't hire a guide, you can go at your own pace for as long as you want (or until the park closes).
The beach inside the park is beautiful and you can take a dip to cool off from walking around the park. The park is closed on Tuesdays, but open every other day from 7:00am until 3:00pm. Pura Vida!
What a wonderful day. Never saw a sloth in the nature. Just amazing.
Vollkommen überlaufen. Wenig Tiere gesehen, obwohl wir früh dort waren. Der Park an sich ist aber schön
very nice park, cool animals, can easily see all 3 types of monkeys between the café and the trail to Playa Gemelas. Great beaches and facilities too
Excelente experiencia. Si hay que venir preparados con agua, buena camara y ganas de caminar. Nuestra guía Beatriz hizo dea visita una gran experiencia

Der Park ist schön ausgebaut, mit Guide sieht man schon einige Tiere. Jedoch extrem überlaufen, sehr viele Menschen überall. VORSICHT auf dem weg dorthin stehen Betrüger, die einem einen Parkplatz aufdrängen wollen auf unschöne Art und Weise! Es gibt einige schönere Touren und Parks.
Muy buen parque, es importante ir con guía porque sino no se ven los animales. Marzo es época seca y la cascada no tieneagus. La playa es espectacular, agua cálida, sin olas
Don’t bring single use plastic, any food or fruits unless for diabetic or special conditions, bottle of water must be metallic
No parking lot. Inside there are the dry-dry ground, because it's the end of the dry season and crowds, and crowds, and crowds. Also a few beaches. But that is not what people're looking for.
Not allowing water in the plastic bottles and selling water for the 9$ inside. #naturesaved
Bello lugar

Mooi park met een mooi strand , wel druk. Als je met de auto komt wel doorrijden tot aan het park. Reeds 1 kilometer van het park vandaan proberen ze je te laten parkeren zodat je vervolgens ver moet lopen om bij het park te komen!
Wunderschön! Es lohnt sich früh da zu sein und mit einem Guide zu gehen.

Navštívit, nenavštívit? Proč ne, ale připravte se velké množství lidí, hlavně v ranních hodinách. Také je tu nepochopitelný systém nahaněčů. To jsou lidi, kteří vám nabízí průvodce parkem. Jisté je to, že musíte platit vstup, který se kupuje online. Pak můžete chodit po parku i bez průvodce, ale pravděpodobně žádné zvíře neuvidíte.

We really enjoyed our visit to this park. Fortunately, it wasn’t very crowded, which made the experience even more special. The place is absolutely beautiful, with spectacular flora and fauna that can be appreciated throughout the entire visit.
We decided to explore without a guide and go at our own pace, which was a great choice. We saw several animals and were able to fully enjoy the peaceful surroundings. Everything is very well maintained, which makes the visit even more pleasant.
Without a doubt, it’s a place we highly recommend visiting if you’re in Costa Rica.
BEAUTIFUL

We came to visit the park. I was expecting to be more natural. It was so for sure over crowded with a big thick walking path. We saw more animals in other area of Costa Rica (I think the amount of people scare them away) The beach is beautiful, Infact it’s a safe rip tide free one so we wanted to come with kids. Unfortunately you are can’t visit the beach again without buying more tickets for another day . At $20 a person that adds up. All and all I would personally skip it next time.

Absolutely stunning national park — easily one of the most beautiful places I’ve visited. The scenery is incredible, with lush greenery, pristine beaches, and wildlife everywhere you look. We saw monkeys, birds, and even some animals we didn’t expect, which made the whole experience feel really special and immersive.

That said, one thing to be aware of: they are very strict about nicotine products. They will confiscate anything like that at the entrance, no exceptions. It caught me a bit off guard, so just be prepared and plan accordingly.

Overall, an amazing place that’s 100% worth visiting — just know the rules ahead of time so there are no surprises.

Wir haben den slot verpasst.

Davor gab es sehr unangenehme Erfahrungen mit den Abzockern

Muito bonito o parque e a praia, mas bastante cheio de gente em época alta. Não permite entrada de nenhum alimento, pode-se comprar no interior. Tem torneiras com água nos percursos para encher a  garrafa de água
Best beach I've ever seen, in the national park. Entry to the park has to be paid in advance, can be done online.
Over crowded. Too many parking guys trying to scam. Nice park though!

Loved the beaches and National Park if we could have just paid without a guide. We were told the $18 tickets were not available for 3 days. If we wanted to go in we needed to book with a guide who would be with us and a group for 2 hours, at a cost os $133 for both of us. I would warn to book in advance. We were in the 0800 group along with masses of people queuing for over half an hour. 3000 people a day go in. The guide took an hour to go 100 yds. We were happy when the tour was over and spotted more wildlife left to ourselves.
Saying that the beaches were beautiful, we saw sloths as usual high in the trees
I agree that no food or plastic bottles should be allowed,bags were searched, as there was not any rubbish.
All this said if we had known and got the normal priced tickets it would have been even better. Others had better guides
Beware it's crowded at the start

This is a beautiful national park.
The jungle and beaches are amazing.
We hired a guide and would highly recommend that. You will have a hard time seeing a lot of the wildlife otherwise.
They know where to look and they use spotting scopes that are superior to trying to find things with binoculars on your own.
They're also very knowledgeable and provide for a fun experience.
But, you can definitely enjoy the park on your own as well. You just won't see some of the harder to spot wildlife without a guide.
Andres was our tour guide, very knowledgeable.  The park was full of wildlife, the beaches were pristine.
We were lucky that we did a Private Tour (Tucanes Tours) and our Guide (Jeremy) was excellent. One of our best half day tours, seeing so much wildlife & fauna
Un paseo con encanto y para disfrutar en la playa
Lots of wildlife that you'll mostly only see if you have a guide. The main trails and beach are very crowded.

Rispetto ad altri parchi visitati ( tipo Tortuguero) questo  è stata una mezza delusione. Una folla all'ingresso e sui sentieri, sembrava di essere in coda per entrare alla Cappella Sistina. Gruppi che si incrociano, bambini che strillano, adulti che chiacchierano come fossero sulla passeggiata a mare di Sanremo. Animali avvistati pochi, un tour fatto a passo di carica, e arrivati alla spiaggia la nostra guida ci congeda indicandoci il sentiero per l'uscita. Ci gira le foto che ha fatto e tanti saluti. Poche le spiegazioni, solo la fretta di andare a recuperare l'infornata di turisti successivi. Per fortuna mentre eravamo sulla spiaggia per i fatti nostri sono arrivate numerose iguana e tante scimmie cappuccine. Non vale la pena venire fino qui, la Costa Rica ha migliori attrazioni.

El parque es hermoso. Ninguno de los caminos fue difícil de recorrer (hasta los que decían que eran de dificultad alta). Lo mejor es ir a la playa temprano a la mañana (tipo 7/8) porque es cuando está prácticamente vacía.

Super beau parc.  Une belle plage côté pacifique! Plein d'animaux a voir.   Ne vous faites pas avoir avec les parkings.  Il y a quelques stationnement gratuit dans la rue près du parc, mais souvent pris.   Il y a des parking payant a proximité.   Continuer jusqu'au parc et suivez les instructions des préposé aux stationnement.   Vous éviterai de payer trop cher et de devoir marcher loins.

Das Erlebnis war nicht so gut. Es gab totales Chaos auf den Straßen und mit den Parkplätzen, eng und hektisch, extrem viele Menschen. Die vermeintlichen Parkwächter werfen sich regelrecht vor die Autos, um sie zu stoppen. Wenn man nicht bei ihnen parkt, dann wird man beschimpft. Wir waren am Morgen dort, und der Park war extrem voll, sowas haben wir wirklich noch nicht gesehen. Wir hatten einen Guide, aber der hat auch nur da geschaut, wo alle anderen waren. Am Eingang sind sehr unfreundliche Menschen, die Taschenkontrollen machen. Man wird dort wie ein Häftling behandelt. Das Restaurant im Park ist ein absolutes No-Go, eingegittert, hier wieder das Gefühl, im Gefängnis zu sein. Der Strand ist wunderschön, aber auch sehr überfüllt. Das Erlebnis kann man sich sparen, es gibt weitaus schönere Spots in Costa Rica.

Nice national park. I recommend to go early morning and stay the whole day. At the beginning of the day, the beaches are empty and you have a higher chance to spot wildlife. Hiring a guide or hitchhiking a group whenever the see something is highly recommended. There is a restaurant to buy food and drinks.

Amazing national park, so nice to be in nature and experience the lush tropical forest and wildlife. We managed to see many animals without a tour guide, though those people can really spot so many more.

In comparison to other parks we found it significantly more crowded.

The beaches were also nice, with a few shaded spots to rest. The smaller one, a little further out was less visited yet the water didn’t seem as clean
Hermoso!!!! Ya quiero volver  a costar rica
Lots of monkeys and other animals. Great beaches with a little bit of snorkeling. Enjoyed our day here.
Mucha gente,por lo que no se puede disfrutar
Muy bueno. No se preocupen por llegar puntual se puede entrar a gualquier hora ya comprada la entrada. Parqueo 4000 colonos.
This park is pristine! The lush jungle, meets beach, meets nature, is magical 🪄
Saw lots of monkeys, and sloths, don’t need to bother with your guide go at your own pace and look around.
